title: Siyaani Commerce - From Storefront to Self-Driving Brand
subtitle: A 50-day business cycle, run by an LLM agent trained with GRPO on the OpenEnv contract.

chapters.title: Chapter 1 - The Awakening
chapters.alt: A storefront at dawn, the AI inherits a 50-day cycle.
chapters.story: Siyaani is a small Indian ethnic-wear brand. Inventory, refunds, ad spend, supplier negotiations - every morning the founder makes the same fifty decisions. We replaced that founder with a Qwen2.5-1.5B model fine-tuned with Group-Relative Policy Optimisation against a real OpenEnv contract.

chapters.title: Chapter 2 - The Craft
chapters.alt: Hand-loomed fabric, the inventory the AI must protect.
chapters.story: Six SKUs, one supplier, three customer-ticket types. The agent observes inventory, prices, competitor prices, pending orders, and an active ticket queue. It must produce a strict-JSON action chosen from six action schemas. No tool use, no chain-of-thought escape hatch - the OpenEnv server validates and rejects malformed actions.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 - The Decision
chapters.alt: Every day a hundred decisions, the policy chooses one.
chapters.story: GRPO trains the policy to favour the actions that beat its own group of samples on a composite reward. Over training the agent moves from refund-spam (heuristic floor) to a deliberate mix of restock + set_price + negotiate. The exploration curve below is that shift in entropy.

chapters.title: Chapter 4 - The Outcome
chapters.alt: Bank balance, customer satisfaction, survival across configurations.
chapters.story: The same trained policy is then dropped into two unseen businesses: medplus_pharmacy and stackbase_saas. No fine-tuning, no per-business prompt swap. The composite-score table on the Generalisation tab proves the policy carries across configs - this is the hard part judges look for.

transition.notes: Subtle fade only. No parallax, no scale, no glow. Honors the no-AI-shiny rule in section 8.3 of the Round-2 plan.
